sound_type: low grunts + clicks -- lower-frequency burst-pulse grunts, broadband echolocation clicks. Less whistle-dominant than Pacific White-sided Dolphin. Deep-water voice. E=4
ecosystem: Deep water, continental shelf edge, submarine canyons. Pelagic. Visits outer Washington coast and Strait of Juan de Fuca.
season: Year-round in eastern North Pacific. Intermittent in Washington waters. Deep-water preference.

coordinates.description: Risso's Dolphin offshore Washington. Continental shelf edge. Deep water. Submarine canyons.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Delphinidae -- dolphin family continues. Monotypic genus (Grampus). Deep-water squid specialist. Scarred appearance ages from gray to white. 10 consecutive marine mammals (180-189) BREAKS Picidae nonet record.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Delphinidae: Pacific White-sided Dolphin (188), Risso's Dolphin (189).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Grampus from Latin/French 'grand poisson' (great fish). griseus from Latin 'griseus' (gray). 'Risso's' after Antoine Risso, French-Italian naturalist who first described the species.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Grampus -- monotypic genus. Only one species: G. griseus.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Fourth cetacean in SPS: Phocoenidae (186-187), Delphinidae (188-189).
taxonomy_note.family_position: Fifth marine mammal family continues: Phocidae (180-181), Otariidae (182-184), Mustelidae (185), Phocoenidae (186-187), Delphinidae (188-189). 10 consecutive marine mammals.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 10 consecutive (180-189). BREAKS PICIDAE NONET RECORD. New longest streak.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 1,000-120,000 Hz. Broad range spanning audible and ultrasonic.
song_metrics.peak_energy_band_hz: 1,000-20,000 Hz (grunts and clicks in lower audible range).
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Grunts: 1-20 kHz. Clicks: 2-120 kHz. Whistles: 4-22 kHz (less prominent).
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Grunts: 0.1-1 sec. Clicks: rapid trains. Whistles: 0.2-1 sec.
song_metrics.note_structure: Low-frequency grunts (burst-pulse, buzzy), broadband clicks (echolocation), occasional whistles.
song_metrics.note_types: Burst-pulse grunts (social), echolocation clicks (deep-water navigation), whistles (contact).
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Less chorus-like than Pacific White-sided Dolphin. More individual vocalizations. Deep-water acoustic environment.
song_metrics.timbre: Grunts: low, buzzy, grunting. Clicks: powerful, broadband. Whistles: tonal but less prominent.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=4. Functional, restrained. Deep-water specialist voice. Lower energy than acrobatic Pacific White-sided Dolphin.
song_metrics.tempo: Variable. Echolocation continuous during foraging. Social grunts intermittent.
song_metrics.context: Deep water, continental shelf edge. Squid foraging, social interaction, deep diving.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Population-level differences in click characteristics documented.
song_metrics.key_property: LOW GRUNTS + CLICKS. Second Delphinidae. Deep-water squid specialist. Scarred appearance. Monotypic genus. 10 consecutive marine mammals (NEW RECORD). E=4. v1.49.330.

pelage.overall: Born dark gray. Accumulates white scarring from squid rakes, tooth rakes, parasites. Older individuals nearly entirely white. Blunt head with no prominent beak. Tall, falcate dorsal fin.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: White scarring on gray body. Blunt head. No beak. Tall dorsal fin. Older = whiter.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Minimal seasonal. Progressive lightening with age (lifetime scale).
pelage.swimming_pattern: Moderate speed. Less acrobatic than Pacific White-sided Dolphin. Occasional breaches. Often shows dorsal fin and back when surfacing.

physical_description.overall: Length: 2.6-3.8 m. Weight: 300-500 kg. Robust body. Blunt head with vertical crease. No prominent beak. Tall falcate dorsal fin. Reduced dentition: 2-7 pairs lower jaw only.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Heavily scarred gray-to-white body. Blunt head. Tall dorsal fin. No beak. Scars increase with age.
physical_description.field_id: Large gray-to-white dolphin with heavy scarring. Blunt head. Tall dorsal fin. Less acrobatic than Pacific White-sided Dolphin. Often in small to moderate groups.

nodes.title: Scarred Body as Living Record
nodes.description: Born gray, ages to white through accumulated scarring from squid, conspecifics, parasites. Each individual uniquely marked. Individual identification via scar patterns.

nodes.title: Deep-Water Squid Specialist
nodes.description: Dives 300-1,000+ m. Squid primary prey. Reduced dentition (suction feeding). Nocturnal foraging follows squid vertical migration.

nodes.title: 10 Consecutive Marine Mammals -- NEW RECORD
nodes.description: Degrees 180-189. BREAKS Picidae nonet record. 5 families. New longest taxonomic streak in engine history.

cross_references.relationship: DELPHINIDAE PAIR! First dolphin (acrobatic, squawks + whistles, E=5) to second dolphin (deep-water, grunts + clicks, E=4). Acrobat to scarred specialist.
STREAK RECORD! 10 consecutive marine mammals (180-189). BREAKS Picidae nonet. 5 families. New longest streak.
THE FOUNDATIONAL! Mudhoney = foundational grunge (38 years). Risso's = deep-water survivor (scarred). Both persist. Total E=11.
